privileged_info_summary: The Oswego dataset is based on real historical data from an actual gastrointestinal illness outbreak that occurred on April 18, 1940, following a church supper in Lycoming, Oswego County, New York. Of 75 persons interviewed, 46 became ill with nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, and abdominal pain. The outbreak was caused by Staphylococcus aureus contamination in vanilla ice cream, which had an 80% attack rate among those who consumed it. The metadata food items list is accurate and matches the 14 foods served at the supper (baked ham, spinach, mashed potatoes, cabbage salad, jello, rolls, brown bread, milk, coffee, water, cakes, vanilla ice cream, chocolate ice cream, and fruit salad). The expected row count after filtering for ill patients (40-50 range) is verified: 46 ill cases fall within this range. The ILL column and 'Yes' filter value are standard encodings used in the Oswego dataset.
